title: CLAUDE.md
text: radiatR R package plotting analyzing animal movement trajectories circular/radial arenas. handles full pipeline: camera calibration → data import → coordinate transformation → circular statistics → visualization.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: commands run R (Rscript). project uses renv dependency isolation — .Rprofile activates automatically.
code: # Install/restore dependencies renv::restore()  # Rebuild documentation from Roxygen2 tags (run before check) devtools::document()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-circular_statistics.R")  # Full R CMD CHECK devtools::check()  # Install locally devtools::install_local(".")

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/TrajSet.R defines central container. TrajSet holds list trajectory tibbles plus metadata (arena geometry, transform history). Almost every downstream function either accepts returns TrajSet. Understand class touching module.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Calibration (R/calibration.R, R/calibration_capture.R) — maps pixel coordinates real-world positions via CalModel S4 object. cam_cal_pt / cam_cal_many build models; calibrate_positions (TrajSet method) applies . Import (R/loaders.R) — largest module. Reads manifest files describe track data lives, dispatches format-specific readers (TrajSet_read_*). loader format registry (register_loader_*) lets callers add new file formats without modifying core code. Coordinate transformation (R/circular_trials.R, R/circular_mapping.R) — converts Cartesian pixel coordinates unit-circle angles. circular_trials.R extracts per-trial segments normalises unit arena; circular_mapping.R handles pixel→angle arithmetic. Heading computation (R/headings.R) — derives directional headings position sequences. Uses rule registry pattern (analogous loader registry) custom heading rules can registered applied name. Statistics (R/circular_statistics.R) — thin wrapper around circular package. circ_summary main entry point. Visualisation (R/circular_plotting.R) — radiate() primary plot function; returns ggplot2 object. Helper functions (add_ticks, add_circ, degree_labs, etc.) return ggplot2 layers designed added +.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: New file formats: use register_loader_read_function / register_loader_info_function (see R/loaders.R). New heading rules: use register_heading_rule (see R/headings.R). registries follow pattern: named list stored package env, dispatched runtime.

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: radiatR uses pinhole + Brown-Conrady model shared MATLAB OpenCV: Focal lengths fx, fy principal point cx, cy, pixels. Radial distortion k1, k2, k3 tangential distortion p1, p2. stored CalModel object, calibrate_positions() applies TrajSet. subtlety worth flagging front: internally radiatR keeps intrinsic matrix MATLAB’s transposed convention — focal lengths diagonal, principal point bottom row (K[3, 1:2]), right-hand column. never assemble matrix hand: cal_model() read_calibration() .

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: F argument sets units corrected output. F left NULL (default) set c(fx, fy), means output stays undistorted pixels — distortion principal-point offset removed, scale unchanged. obtain metric result instead, pass physical focal length millimetres:

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: practice export calibration another tool read read_calibration(), auto-detects format file extension:
code: # MATLAB Computer Vision Toolbox (.mat) model <- read_calibration("camera_params.mat")  # OpenCV FileStorage (.yml / .yaml / .json) model <- read_calibration("opencv_calib.yml")  # Plain CSV (wide or parameter/value) model <- read_calibration("calib.csv")

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: Save relevant fields cameraParameters / cameraIntrinsics object .mat file. radiatR recognises pre-R2022b IntrinsicMatrix (already transposed convention) newer standard K, alongside RadialDistortion TangentialDistortion. Reading .mat files needs R.matlab package. MATLAB reports principal point 1-based pixels. read_calibration() subtracts one default (principal_base = 1) lines 0-based coordinates produced tracking tools. Override principal_base tracker uses different convention.
code: % in MATLAB, after calibrating params = cameraParams; IntrinsicMatrix     = params.IntrinsicMatrix; RadialDistortion    = params.RadialDistortion; TangentialDistortion = params.TangentialDistortion; save('camera_params.mat', 'IntrinsicMatrix', ...      'RadialDistortion', 'TangentialDistortion');

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: cv::FileStorage writes camera_matrix distortion_coefficients (order k1, k2, p1, p2, k3). radiatR reads YAML JSON variants tolerates non-standard %YAML:1.0 header !!opencv-matrix tags. YAML needs yaml package; JSON needs jsonlite. OpenCV principal points already 0-based, shift applied default.

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: coefficients typed hand, CSV works either layout — wide: long (parameter,value rows). Missing coefficients default zero. CSV principal points treated 0-based.

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: single landmark, cam_cal_pt() applies model one pixel coordinate; cam_cal_many() table points one pass: point principal point (640, 360) maps origin; others recentred, undistorted, scaled F.

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: usual entry point calibrate_positions(), corrects every track TrajSet, rewrites x/y columns place, re-derives angle corrected geometry. bundled millipede example provides real pixel-space tracks camera circular arena. real workflow, replace illustrative model one imported via read_calibration() calibration captured camera lens.

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: Removing distortion requires inverting distortion model, radiatR fixed-point solver (OpenCV’s undistortPoints ). defaults (max_iterations = 5, tolerance = 1e-9) usually ample. extreme distortion points far optical axis can relax : convergence fails function returns last iterate; check coefficients tighten stopping criteria.
code: cam_cal_pt(650, 380, model@K, model@k, model@F,            max_iterations = 10, tolerance = 1e-12)

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: radiatR imports calibrations; estimate . Calibrate MATLAB, OpenCV, another tool, bring coefficients . read_calibration() reads MATLAB .mat, OpenCV YAML/JSON, plain CSV; cal_model() builds model coefficients already hold. Apply resulting CalModel single points (cam_cal_pt()), tables (cam_cal_many()), whole trajectory sets (calibrate_positions()). Mind conventions: principal-point pixel base (1-based MATLAB vs 0-based OpenCV) F scale factor (undistorted pixels default, metric pass physical focal length).

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: main radiatR vignette covers path raw tracking files plotted set headings. vignette picks heading data frame shows analysis layer: Dispersion summaries — circ_dispersion(), sector_summary() Parametric fits — vonmises_fit(), wrappedcauchy_fit() Hypothesis tests — test_uniformity(), test_mean_directions(), test_concentration(), multiple-comparison correction Correlation — circ_cor() (circular-linear circular-circular) Distribution overlays — add_angle_rose(), add_vonmises_density(), add_wrappedcauchy_density(), add_circular_kde() Significance geometry — add_critical_r() (Rayleigh / V-test circle) add_critical_v_line() (V-test boundary) Every statistics function takes data frame heading column radians (default name "heading") returns tidy data frame, results drop straight dplyr, knitr::kable(), plotting.

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: derive one heading per trial bundled cpunctatus dataset ring-crossing rule, attach trial’s target half-width (arc) grouping. construction used main vignette. heading column radians, reference-relative (0 = toward target). Everything operates column.
code: data(cpunctatus)  hd <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "crossing",                       circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                       coords = "relative",                       angle_convention = "clock") names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id"  # attach target half-width (arc) from the dataset, by trial arc_map  <- unique(cpunctatus@data[, c("trial_id", "arc")]) hd       <- merge(hd, arc_map, by = "trial_id") hd$arc   <- factor(hd$arc)  # keep trials with a defined crossing heading hd <- hd[is.finite(hd$heading), , drop = FALSE] head(hd[, c("trial_id", "arc", "heading")]) #>   trial_id arc   heading #> 1   10_1_1  10 0.2777794 #> 2  10_10_1  10 3.0376774 #> 3  10_11_1  10 6.0710737 #> 4  10_12_1  10 2.2074837 #> 5  10_13_1  10 0.9322361 #> 6  10_14_1  10 4.7955587

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: circ_dispersion() returns mean direction, resultant length R, circular standard deviation. Grouping arc gives one row per condition. R runs 0 (uniformly scattered) 1 (headings identical); circular SD moves opposite way. dense per-frame heading series — gaze direction tethered animal, say — sector_summary() bins angles reports dwell proportions per sector:

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: vonmises_fit() estimates mean direction μ\mu concentration κ\kappa maximum likelihood, asymptotic standard errors confidence interval μ\mu: wrappedcauchy_fit() heavier-tailed alternative — robust data outliers weak directionality. concentration ρ\rho bounded [0,1)[0, 1): return row per group, quick merge() puts two concentration estimates side side comparison.

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: test_uniformity() asks, per group, whether headings preferred direction. Rayleigh test gives exact p-value; testing many conditions , pass p_adjust corrected p_value_adj column:

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: test_mean_directions() Watson-Williams test — circular analogue one-way ANOVA mean angle. omnibus form asks whether group differs: Set pairwise = TRUE pairwise comparisons; p_adjust strongly recommended number comparisons grows quickly:
code: test_mean_directions(hd, group_col = "arc") #>   n_groups statistic df1 df2     p_value            test #> 1        8  8.230315   7 218 6.70879e-09 Watson-Williams pw <- test_mean_directions(hd, group_col = "arc",                            pairwise = TRUE, p_adjust = "holm") head(pw[order(pw$p_value_adj), ]) #>    group1 group2 statistic df1 df2      p_value            test  p_value_adj #> 14     20     30  68.71016   1  56 2.590906e-11 Watson-Williams 7.254536e-10 #> 22     30      0  45.35938   1  65 5.086470e-09 Watson-Williams 1.373347e-07 #> 6      10     50  31.92401   1  57 5.341214e-07 Watson-Williams 1.388716e-05 #> 4      10     40  24.34873   1  52 8.658990e-06 Watson-Williams 2.164747e-04 #> 1      10     15  16.68434   1  60 1.328071e-04 Watson-Williams 3.187369e-03 #> 13     15      0  16.32869   1  58 1.586887e-04 Watson-Williams 3.649841e-03

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: test_concentration() checks whether groups equally concentrated (circular analogue test equal variances), key assumption behind Watson-Williams test :
code: test_concentration(hd, group_col = "arc") #>   statistic df    p_value        test #> 1  14.23259  7 0.04719587 equal.kappa

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: circ_cor() measures association headings covariate. x_type = "linear" (default) computes circular-linear correlation — , whether heading direction associated numeric target half-width: returned r unsigned (association strength, 0–1); test statistic nr2n r^2 approximately χ22\chi^2_2. two angular variables, pass x_type = "circular" get Fisher’s ρ∈[−1,1]\rho \[-1, 1].
code: hd$arc_num <- as.numeric(as.character(hd$arc)) circ_cor(hd, x_col = "arc_num", angle_col = "heading", x_type = "linear") #>           r   n            type statistic df    p_value #> 1 0.2175965 226 circular-linear   10.7007  2 0.00474648

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: overlay layers draw angular distribution Cartesian unit-disc space radiate(), compose onto bare circular canvas +. build canvas : rose diagram bins headings wedges; parametric non-parametric density curves overlay top. Giving every layer scale aligns radii, shapes can compared directly:  grey wedges empirical rose; steelblue curve von Mises fit, darkorange wrapped Cauchy, tomato circular kernel density estimate. parametric curves track rose closely fit good; systematic gap (especially tails) cue prefer heavier-tailed wrapped Cauchy.
code: canvas <- ggplot() + coord_fixed() +   add_circ(radius = 1) +   theme_void() vm <- vonmises_fit(hd)        # pooled fit (group_col = NULL) wc <- wrappedcauchy_fit(hd)  canvas +   add_angle_rose(hd, bins = 12, scale = 0.8, fill = "grey80") +   add_circular_kde(hd, scale = 0.8, colour = "tomato") +   add_vonmises_density(vm, scale = 0.8, colour = "steelblue") +   add_wrappedcauchy_density(wc, scale = 0.8, colour = "darkorange")

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: remaining two helpers draw decision boundary significance test directly resultant-length space (radius 0–1), can read observed mean vector. add_critical_r() draws Rayleigh critical circle: mean resultant length needed significance level alpha, namely rcrit=−log⁡(α)/nr_\text{crit} = \sqrt{-\log(\alpha)/n}. mean vector reaches past circle, headings significantly non-uniform.  arrow tip lies well outside dashed critical circle, matching tiny Rayleigh p-value . add_critical_v_line() corresponding boundary V-test, tests uniformity specified direction mu0 (0, .e. toward target). Unlike Rayleigh circle, V-test boundary straight line perpendicular mu0: significance requires mean vector’s projection onto mu0 exceed c=zα/2nc = z_\alpha / \sqrt{2n}. Set show_region = TRUE shade rejection side.  experiment priori expected direction (target), V-test powerful omnibus Rayleigh test, line makes one-sided nature decision visible.
code: disp <- circ_dispersion(hd) mean_vec <- data.frame(x = disp$resultant_R * cos(disp$mean_dir),                        y = disp$resultant_R * sin(disp$mean_dir))  canvas +   add_critical_r(hd, alpha = 0.05, test = "rayleigh") +   geom_segment(data = mean_vec,                aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = x, yend = y),                arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "inches")),                linewidth = 1) test_uniformity(hd, test = "rayleigh") #>   statistic    p_value   n     test #> 1 0.1298181 0.02217652 226 rayleigh canvas +   add_critical_v_line(hd, mu0 = 0, alpha = 0.05, show_region = TRUE) +   geom_segment(data = mean_vec,                aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = x, yend = y),                arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "inches")),                linewidth = 1)

text: radiatR reads tabular (CSV/TSV) exports many tracking tools named dialects. dialect maps tool’s column conventions onto TrajSet model one position per individual per frame. Pass dialect name TrajSet_read(path, dialect = "name"); tool-specific options go dialect_args. table summarises currently handled. Two limitations general: Tabular text . Dialects read CSV/TSV (, optional packages installed, JSON/XML/Parquet). Native binary formats — NumPy .npz MATLAB .mat — read except noted (ctrax reads .mat via R.matlab package). Position, posture. radiatR models single position per individual per frame. code: fake_json <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") jsonlite::write_json(   list(     tracks = list(       A = list(timestamp = c(0, 1, 2), px = c(0, 1, 2), py = c(0, 0.2, 0.5)),       B = list(timestamp = c(0, 1, 2), px = c(1, 1.3, 1.5), py = c(0, -0.1, -0.2))     )   ),   fake_json,   auto_unbox = TRUE )  json_tracks_fn <- function(x) {   dat <- if (is.character(x) && file.exists(x)) jsonlite::fromJSON(x) else x   entries <- purrr::imap(dat$tracks, function(track, id) {     data.frame(       id   = id,       time = track$timestamp,       x    = track$px,       y    = track$py,       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   })   do.call(rbind, entries) } register_loader_dialect("json_tracks", json_tracks_fn) ts_json <- TrajSet_read(json_tracks_fn(fake_json)) ts_json #> TrajSet: 2 trajectories, 6 observations #> Columns: id='id', time='time', angle='angle' (radians), x='x', y='y', raw_x='x_raw', raw_y='y_raw' #>     id time         x          y x_raw y_raw rho     angle #> A.1  A    0 0.0000000  0.0000000   0.0   0.0  NA 0.0000000 #> A.2  A    1 0.9805807  0.1961161   1.0   0.2   1 0.1973956 #> A.3  A    2 0.9701425  0.2425356   2.0   0.5   1 0.2449787 #> B.1  B    0 1.0000000  0.0000000   1.0   0.0   1 0.0000000 #> B.2  B    1 0.9970545 -0.0766965   1.3  -0.1   1 6.2064134 #> B.3  B    2 0.9912279 -0.1321637   1.5  -0.2   1 6.1506338

title: radiatR
text: radiatR streamlines journey raw tracking files publication-ready plots experiments conducted circular arenas. package includes: utilities importing paired landmark/track text files produced popular tracking suites; helpers extracting trial limits computing circular summary statistics; polished ggplot2 layers drawing concentric guides annotated paths.

title: radiatR
text: package ships full set millipede example tracks demonstrate import workflow. Landmark files (_point01.txt) record two pixel-space reference points per trial (arena centre target location arena wall); track files (_point02.txt) contain full xy trajectory. get_all_object_pos() reads landmark/track pair, normalises coordinates unit circle (arena radius = 1), returns TrajSet. Trial metadata (arena radius, target position, frame limits) ts_demo@meta$trial_limits.

title: radiatR

title: radiatR
text: radiate() draws trajectories unit circle concentric reference rings. Colouring arc factor shows paths cluster condition.

title: radiatR
text: crossing method — projecting vector two concentric-ring crossings unit circle — assigns one directional heading per trial. derive_headings() return_coords = TRUE returns heading angle inner-ring crossing position. Overlaying heading endpoints (one hollow circle per trial) grand mean direction combined trajectory plot gives compact summary full dataset:  grand mean arrow points 283.2° relative reference direction (clock convention; 0° = toward reference) R = 0.08, reflecting overall reference-relative tendency across conditions.
code: hd <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "crossing",                       circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                       return_coords = TRUE) names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id" # join the target half-width (arc) from the dataset for grouping/faceting arc_map <- unique(cpunctatus@data[, c("trial_id", "arc")]) hd       <- merge(hd, arc_map, by = "trial_id") hd$arc   <- factor(hd$arc) attr(hd, "colour_col") <- "arc" attr(hd, "display")    <- circ_display(zero = 0) head(hd[, c("trial_id", "arc", "heading", "x_inner", "y_inner")]) #>   trial_id arc   heading     x_inner     y_inner #> 1   10_1_1  10 0.3545811  0.19556350 -0.04102813 #> 2  10_10_1  10 6.2337825 -0.03715972  0.19558015 #> 3  10_11_1  10 1.3353505 -0.07231344  0.18624466 #> 4  10_12_1  10 2.2846471 -0.18291981  0.08084798 #> 5  10_13_1  10 5.6521348  0.18223038  0.08232129 #> 6  10_14_1  10 1.6589765  0.09025066  0.17836615 p_all <- radiate(cpunctatus,                  group_col   = "trial_id",                  colour_col  = "arc",                  show_labels = FALSE,                  show_arrow  = FALSE,                  display     = circ_display(zero = 0)) +   add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "arc", size = 1, alpha = 0.6)  p_all + add_heading_arrow(hd) #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: radiatR

code: iv <- compute_circ_interval(hd, colour_col = "arc", stat = "bootstrap_ci") # replace with Bayesian posteriors: iv$lower <- ...; iv$upper <- ... add_circ_interval(iv, colour_col = "arc")

title: radiatR

Trajectories:  + Heading endpoints trial’s crossing location:  + Grand mean direction arrow:  arc radius 1.05 spans 95 % bootstrap confidence interval grand mean direction pooled across arc conditions.

title: radiatR
text: Three strategies control trajectories coloured. Option 1 — single colour. Pass colour string directly heading-overlay helpers; omit colour_col radiate() draw tracks default grey. Option 2 — cycling palette. colour_cycle assigns trajectory colour index cycles back 1 every n trajectories. panel_by set cycle restarts independently within panel trajectory 1 every panel always gets colour 1. Pass integer automatic palette assignment, character vector specify colours explicitly. Option 3 — variable mapping. colour_col maps data column colour (e.g. arc factor used combined-trajectory plot ).

title: radiatR
text: Faceting arc angle shows condition separately. Within panel, assign_cycle_colours() distinguishes individual trajectories cycling 10 colours, resetting panel boundary. Calling explicitly track data headings data frame (joining trial id) means heading markers inherit exact per-trajectory colour — single per-condition colour — colour_col used .  + Bootstrap CI arc added first sits behind heading markers:  + Heading vectors (dotted lines inner crossing perimeter):  + Heading points top CI arc:   panel shows trajectories heading markers coloured per-trajectory cycling palette, bootstrap CI arc radius 1.05 condition colour (rendered behind heading points), solid mean direction arrow, degree labels. clock convention (0° = toward reference, clockwise). Use strip_position = "inside" place label inside plot area, strip_labels = FALSE suppress .
code: # Pre-compute cycling colours: 10 colours, restarting within each arc panel. # Join to headings so heading layers can use the same column. cpunctatus_cc        <- cpunctatus cpunctatus_cc@data   <- assign_cycle_colours(cpunctatus@data,                                             id_col    = "trial_id",                                             n         = 10,                                             panel_col = "arc") colour_map <- unique(cpunctatus_cc@data[, c("trial_id", "cycle_colour")]) hd_cc      <- merge(hd, colour_map,                     by      = "trial_id", all.x = TRUE) attr(hd_cc, "display")    <- attr(hd, "display", exact = TRUE) attr(hd_cc, "colour_col") <- "cycle_colour" p <- radiate(cpunctatus_cc,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "cycle_colour",         panel_by     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE,         display      = circ_display(zero = 0)) p p <- p + add_heading_interval(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black",                                stat = "bootstrap_ci", boot_reps = 999L) p p <- p + add_heading_vectors(hd_cc) p #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`). p <- p + add_heading_points(hd_cc, size = 4) p #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`). #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). p <- p + add_heading_arrow(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black") p #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`). #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: radiatR
text: Three functions handle directional density overlays; designed compose cleanly density source: Three built-methods available compute_circular_density(): "vonmises" (MLE via circular::mle.vonmises()), "kernel" (circular KDE), "histogram" (bin counts). computation rendering steps separate, density column can replaced values model — including Bayesian posterior predictive density brms — calling add_circular_density(): convenience wrapper add_heading_density() skips intermediate step substitution needed. combined panel plot uses shade per-condition von Mises density alongside heading vectors:  shaded region von Mises density fitted crossing headings within arc condition. narrower, taller peak indicates concentrated directional responses. Switch method = "kernel" non-parametric estimate, method = "histogram" raw count display.
code: dens_df <- compute_circular_density(hd, colour_col = "arc", method = "vonmises") # replace with Bayesian posterior: dens_df$density <- my_brms_fitted_density(dens_df$theta) add_circular_density(dens_df, colour_col = "arc", fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35) radiate(cpunctatus_cc,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "cycle_colour",         panel_by     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE,         display      = circ_display(zero = 0)) +   add_heading_density(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc",                       method = "vonmises", scale = 0.4,                       fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35) +   add_heading_vectors(hd_cc) +   add_heading_arrow(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black") #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`).

title: radiatR
text: compute_circular_density() boot_reps > 0 runs non-parametric bootstrap: heading samples drawn replacement, von Mises MLE fitted replicate, density re-evaluated angular grid. boot_alpha / 2 1 - boot_alpha / 2 quantiles across replicates become density_lower density_upper columns, add_circular_density() renders shaded band around fitted curve.  darker band 95% bootstrap confidence interval fitted von Mises density. wider band reflects greater parametric uncertainty, typically seen smaller diffuse conditions. density_lower density_upper columns dens_boot can replaced interval values Bayesian model (e.g. 2.5th 97.5th percentiles brms posterior predictive distribution) passing add_circular_density().
code: dens_boot <- compute_circular_density(hd, colour_col = "arc",                                       method = "vonmises",                                       boot_reps = 499L, boot_alpha = 0.05,                                       n_theta = 300L)  radiate(cpunctatus,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE,         display      = circ_display(zero = 0)) +   add_circular_density(dens_boot, colour_col = "arc",                        scale = 0.4, fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35,                        ci_fill = "grey60", ci_alpha = 0.35) +   add_heading_arrow(hd, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black")

title: radiatR
text: compute_circ_mean() returns per-condition statistics behind arrows : within-trial path consistency (tortuosity), circ_summary() operates step--step angle distribution: High resultant lengths (close 1) indicate consistent step directions within trial.

title: radiatR
text: derive_headings() supports fourteen built-rules. "crossing" well suited echinoderm-style tracks — moderate tortuosity, consistent outward movement — rules may appropriate depending taxon experimental design. Use list_heading_rules() see available names; custom rules can added register_heading_rule(). Two parameter-free alternatives especially useful:

title: radiatR
text: distal rule takes atan2(y, x) frame animal farthest arena centre. requires ring parameters never returns NA every trial -distal frame. Per-condition resultant lengths "distal" close "crossing", consistent millipede tracks fairly direct: Visualising distal headings per-condition panel confirms spatial pattern consistent crossing method:  millipede tracks nearly radial, heading estimates agree closely across methods. sinuous species (moths, pigeons) choice rule larger influence result.
code: hd_distal <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "distal",                               return_coords = TRUE) names(hd_distal)[names(hd_distal) == "id"] <- "trial_id" # join the target half-width (arc) from the dataset, as for the crossing rule hd_distal     <- merge(hd_distal, arc_map, by = "trial_id") hd_distal$arc <- factor(hd_distal$arc) attr(hd_distal, "display") <- circ_display(zero = 0) sm_cross  <- compute_circ_mean(hd,       colour_col = "arc")[, c("arc", "resultant_R")] sm_distal <- compute_circ_mean(hd_distal, colour_col = "arc")[, c("arc", "resultant_R")] names(sm_cross)[2]  <- "crossing" names(sm_distal)[2] <- "distal" merge(sm_cross, sm_distal, by = "arc") #>   arc   crossing     distal #> 1   0 0.21852376 0.23531457 #> 2  10 0.27298867 0.07055967 #> 3  15 0.25745294 0.24477975 #> 4  20 0.10968390 0.07326231 #> 5  30 0.16285026 0.12936762 #> 6  40 0.12904693 0.06536414 #> 7   5 0.03518184 0.07181952 #> 8  50 0.09148952 0.15790182 cpunctatus_cc_d        <- cpunctatus cpunctatus_cc_d@data   <- assign_cycle_colours(cpunctatus@data,                                               id_col    = "trial_id",                                               n         = 10,                                               panel_col = "arc") colour_map_d <- unique(cpunctatus_cc_d@data[, c("trial_id", "cycle_colour")]) hd_distal_cc <- merge(hd_distal, colour_map_d, by = "trial_id", all.x = TRUE) attr(hd_distal_cc, "display")    <- attr(hd_distal, "display", exact = TRUE) attr(hd_distal_cc, "colour_col") <- "cycle_colour"  radiate(cpunctatus_cc_d,         group_col   = "trial_id",         colour_col  = "cycle_colour",         panel_by    = "arc",         ncol        = 3,         show_labels = FALSE,         show_arrow  = FALSE,         display     = circ_display(zero = 0)) +   add_heading_interval(hd_distal_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black",                        stat = "bootstrap_ci", boot_reps = 999L) +   add_heading_points(hd_distal_cc, size = 4) +   add_heading_arrow(hd_distal_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black")

title: radiatR
text: net rule returns direction vector first recorded position last, ignoring intermediate points. fastest possible estimate natural match GPS vanishing-bearing studies release final observed positions available. tracks three methods give similar resultant lengths. Larger differences expected species complex search behaviour committing direction.
code: hd_net <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "net") names(hd_net)[names(hd_net) == "id"] <- "trial_id" # join the target half-width (arc) from the dataset, as for the crossing rule hd_net     <- merge(hd_net, arc_map, by = "trial_id") hd_net$arc <- factor(hd_net$arc)  sm_net <- compute_circ_mean(hd_net, colour_col = "arc")[, c("arc", "resultant_R")] names(sm_net)[2] <- "net" Reduce(function(a, b) merge(a, b, by = "arc"),        list(sm_cross, sm_distal, sm_net)) #>   arc   crossing     distal        net #> 1   0 0.21852376 0.23531457 0.22962978 #> 2  10 0.27298867 0.07055967 0.05152922 #> 3  15 0.25745294 0.24477975 0.23894631 #> 4  20 0.10968390 0.07326231 0.02827464 #> 5  30 0.16285026 0.12936762 0.11498421 #> 6  40 0.12904693 0.06536414 0.06850022 #> 7   5 0.03518184 0.07181952 0.05236747 #> 8  50 0.09148952 0.15790182 0.15353222

title: radiatR
text: simulate_tracks() generates synthetic trajectories without files, useful testing demonstrations. kappa parameter controls directedness:  left panel (low kappa) shows tortuous paths; right (high kappa) shows straighter, directed tracks. mean resultant arrow computed independently per panel.
code: sim_df <- simulate_tracks(   conditions = data.frame(n_trials = c(5L, 5L), kappa = c(2, 8)),   n_points = 150,   seed = 42 ) ts_sim <- TrajSet(   sim_df,   id = "trial_id", time = "frame",   angle = "rel_theta", x = "rel_x", y = "rel_y",   angle_unit = "radians", normalize_xy = FALSE ) radiate(ts_sim,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 5,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 2,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: simulate_tracks() generates synthetic circular-arena trajectories without tracking files. main uses : Verifying new analysis pipeline works real data arrives. Reproducing plots examples documentation. Teaching: demonstrating concentration, tortuosity, directional bias affect appearance paths. Informal power analysis: generate data plausible effect size check whether expected statistical pattern visible.

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Called arguments, simulate_tracks() returns tibble three conditions (60 trials total, 200 frames ): row one frame. key columns :
code: sim <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1) dim(sim) #> [1] 12000    16 names(sim) #>  [1] "condition"     "trial_id"      "trial_num"     "frame"         #>  [5] "predictor"     "concentration" "tortuosity"    "ref_heading"   #>  [9] "final_heading" "rho"           "abs_theta"     "rel_theta"     #> [13] "abs_x"         "abs_y"         "rel_x"         "rel_y"

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: output argument controls returned. output = "trajset" path wraps tibble TrajSet (absolute coordinates, normalisation). Use whenever want plug straight plotting analysis functions.
code: # Default: long-form tibble sim_tbl  <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "tibble")  # TrajSet directly (ready for radiate(), derive_headings(), etc.) sim_ts   <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "trajset")  # Both representations in a named list sim_both <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "both") names(sim_both)  # "tibble" "trajset"

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: three default conditions differ directional bias, concentration, tortuosity (see ).
code: ts_default <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "trajset") radiate(ts_default,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: simulation behaviour controlled data frame passed conditions. conditions = NULL function fills three-condition template. Supply data frame override subset columns; missing columns receive sensible defaults.

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: concentration_base von Mises κ passed rvonmises(). Higher values produce tightly clustered headings; values near zero produce near-uniform heading distributions.  κ = 1 mean arrow short (low resultant length); κ = 12 nearly trials point direction.
code: conds_kappa <- data.frame(   condition          = c("low (κ=0.1)", "medium (κ=1)", "high (κ=12)"),   n_trials           = 20L,   concentration_base = c(0.1, 1, 12),   tortuosity_base    = 0.05 ) ts_kappa <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_kappa, seed = 42,                              output = "trajset") radiate(ts_kappa,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Two parameters control within-trial path shape: tortuosity_base — SD per-step angular noise. Larger values produce sinuous paths. phi — autocorrelation successive angular deviations (0 = white noise, values near 1 produce smooth, sweeping curves). Passed directly simulate_tracks(), via conditions table.
code: conds_tort <- data.frame(   condition          = c("smooth (φ=0.95)", "moderate (φ=0.7)", "noisy (φ=0)"),   n_trials           = 15L,   concentration_base = 6,   tortuosity_base    = c(0.12, 0.12, 0.12) ) phi_vals <- c(0.95, 0.7, 0)  # Simulate each phi separately and bind parts <- lapply(seq_along(phi_vals), function(i) {   d <- conds_tort[i, , drop = FALSE]   simulate_tracks(conditions = d, n_points = 200,                   phi = phi_vals[i], seed = 7 + i) }) ts_tort <- TrajSet(   do.call(rbind, parts),   id = "trial_id", time = "frame",   x = "abs_x", y = "abs_y", angle = "abs_theta",   angle_unit = "radians", normalize_xy = FALSE ) radiate(ts_tort,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: ref_mean shifts reference direction away East (0 rad). Use simulate experiments reference along positive x-axis.
code: conds_bias <- data.frame(   condition          = c("East (0°)", "North (90°)", "Northwest (135°)"),   n_trials           = 20L,   concentration_base = 5,   tortuosity_base    = 0.06,   ref_mean          = c(0, pi / 2, 3 * pi / 4) ) ts_bias <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_bias, seed = 99,                            output = "trajset") radiate(ts_bias,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: common design monotone gradient cue strength. example simulates four levels increasing concentration decreasing tortuosity — mimicking orientation experiment stronger cues produce tighter, smoother paths.
code: conds_grad <- data.frame(   condition          = paste0(c(15, 30, 60, 120), "° arc"),   n_trials           = 25L,   concentration_base = c(1.5, 3, 6, 10),   tortuosity_base    = c(0.14, 0.10, 0.06, 0.03),   tortuosity_sd      = 0.015 ) ts_grad <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_grad, n_points = 200,                             seed = 55, output = "trajset") radiate(ts_grad,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "condition",         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 4,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: concentration_slope tortuosity_slope non-zero, final kappa tortuosity trial depend sampled predictor value. lets model experiments individual differences (e.g. body size, prior exposure) modulate response. Plotting concentration predictor confirms linear relationship:
code: conds_pred <- data.frame(   condition            = "gradient",   n_trials             = 40L,   concentration_base   = 3,   concentration_slope  = 4,    # kappa rises with predictor   tortuosity_base      = 0.10,   tortuosity_slope     = -0.04, # smoother at high predictor   predictor_mean       = 0,   predictor_sd         = 1 ) sim_pred <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_pred, seed = 7, output = "both")  # Each trial gets its own predictor sample range(sim_pred$tibble$predictor) #> [1] -1.387996  2.716752 range(sim_pred$tibble$concentration) #> [1]  0.10000 13.86701 trial_summary <- unique(sim_pred$tibble[, c("trial_id", "predictor",                                              "concentration")]) ggplot(trial_summary, aes(predictor, concentration)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(x = "Predictor", y = "Effective κ") +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Simulated TrajSet objects feed directly derive_headings() circ_summarise(), making straightforward run analysis pipeline synthetic data. Visualise headings alongside trajectories:
code: conds_analysis <- data.frame(   condition          = c("diffuse", "concentrated"),   n_trials           = 30L,   concentration_base = c(1.5, 8),   tortuosity_base    = c(0.12, 0.04) ) sim_analysis <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_analysis, seed = 21,                                  output = "both")  hd <- derive_headings(sim_analysis$trajset, rule = "crossing",                        circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                        coords = "absolute",                        angle_convention = "unit_circle",                        return_coords = TRUE) names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id"  # derive_headings returns only id/heading/coords; join condition back cond_map <- unique(sim_analysis$tibble[, c("trial_id", "condition")]) hd <- merge(hd, cond_map, by = "trial_id")  compute_circ_mean(hd, colour_col = "condition")[,   c("condition", "mean_dir", "resultant_R")] #>      condition   mean_dir resultant_R #> 1 concentrated 0.05301978   0.9039690 #> 2      diffuse 0.29012285   0.7131608 p <- radiate(sim_analysis$trajset,              group_col    = "trial_id",              colour_col   = "condition",              panel_by     = "condition",              ncol         = 2,              show_labels  = FALSE,              show_arrow   = FALSE) +   add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "condition", size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +   add_heading_arrow(hd, colour_col = "condition", colour = "black") p

title: Add a critical resultant-length circle to a radiate plot — add_critical_r
text: Two tests supported: "rayleigh" (default) Tests uniformity     hypothesised direction.  Critical value     \(\bar R_{crit} = \sqrt{-\log(\alpha) / n}\) (asymptotic; accurate     \(n \ge 10\)). "vtest" Tests specific direction.  Critical value     \(\bar R_{crit} = z_\alpha / \sqrt{2n}\) powerful (    observed mean direction equals hypothesised \(\mu_0\));     effective threshold rises observed direction departs     \(\mu_0\), circle lower bound. Sample size n taken per group hd.  group_col matches parent radiate(panel_by = ...) argument, one circle drawn per panel radius appropriate panel's n.  groups overlaid single panel, set per_group = TRUE draw one circle per group (colour-matched), per_group = FALSE (default) draw single conservative circle using smallest n (largest critical radius).

title: Build a camera calibration model from intrinsic coefficients — cal_model
text: radiatR stores intrinsic matrix MATLAB's *transposed* convention: focal lengths sit diagonal (`K[1, 1]`, `K[2, 2]`) principal point occupies bottom row (`K[3, 1:2]`). `cal_model()` owns layout callers never assemble `K` hand. Axis skew used downstream correction therefore dropped. distortion coefficients follow Brown-Conrady model shared MATLAB (`RadialDistortion` + `TangentialDistortion`) OpenCV (`[k1, k2, p1, p2, k3]`): radial terms `k1`, `k2`, `k3` tangential terms `p1`, `p2`.

title: Circular correlation between headings and a covariate — circ_cor
text: Circular-linear (T-linear association, Mardia Jupp 2000): $$r^2 = (r_{cx}^2 + r_{cy}^2 - 2 r_{cx} r_{cy} r_{xy}) / (1 - r_{xy}^2)$$ \(r_{cx}\), \(r_{cy}\), \(r_{xy}\) Pearson correlations \(x\) \(\cos\theta\) \(\sin\theta\), \(\cos\theta\) \(\sin\theta\). \(r\) lies \([0, 1]\); test statistic \(n r^2\) approximately chi-squared 2 degrees freedom null. Note: \(r\) unsigned (association strength , direction). Circular-circular (Fisher's \(\rho\), via cor.circular): \(r \[-1, 1]\).

text: data frame columns `theta` (radians, -pi pi) `density`   (non-negative), plus `colour_col` supplied. `boot_reps > 0`   `method = "vonmises"`, also includes `density_lower` `density_upper`.   Suitable passing [add_circular_density()].

title: Compute a circular density data frame from heading observations — compute_circular_density
text: Three built-estimation methods provided: * `"vonmises"` – fit von Mises distribution MLE   ([circular::mle.vonmises()]) evaluate fitted density regular   grid `n_theta` angles. Bootstrap confidence bands available via   `boot_reps`. * `"kernel"` – circular kernel density estimate   ([circular::density.circular()]) bandwidth chosen   [circular::bw.nrd.circular()] unless `bw` supplied. * `"histogram"` – angular bin counts (circular rose diagram); `bins`   controls number bins. `colour_col` supplied density computed independently group group label preserved output, enabling per-panel use [radiate()]'s `panel_by`. `boot_reps > 0` `method = "vonmises"`, non-parametric bootstrap run: `boot_reps` samples drawn replacement, von Mises MLE fitted , density evaluated grid. `boot_alpha / 2` `1 - boot_alpha / 2` quantiles across replicates returned `density_lower` `density_upper` columns. can rendered confidence band [add_circular_density()], replaced interval values Bayesian model plotting.

what: radiatR: Analysis and Visualisation of Circular Arena Tracking Data — radiatR-package
title: radiatR: Analysis and Visualisation of Circular Arena Tracking Data — radiatR-package
text: complete pipeline analysing animal orientation movement circular arenas. Reads data 20+ tracking tools (EthoVision, DeepLabCut, SLEAP, TRex, -maze, TrackMate, idtracker.ai, Ctrax, others) via unified dialect-based loader system, including multi-bodypart pose estimation likelihood-weighted centroid. Derives per-trial heading directions using multiple rules (ring-crossing, distal point, pose body-axis, velocity-based, ). Computes circular statistics including mean direction, resultant length, von Mises wrapped Cauchy parametric fits, circular correlation (circular-linear circular-circular), multi-sample hypothesis tests (Watson-Williams, Rayleigh, others) multiple-comparison correction. Renders publication-quality ggplot2 track plots overlaid rose diagrams, parametric density curves, non-parametric kernel density estimates. Includes browser-based graphical interface requiring R coding via launch_app().

title: Simulate trajectory sets under configurable experimental conditions — simulate_tracks
text: `conditions` data frame can contain following columns (defaults supplied missing): - `condition` (character): condition label. - `n_trials` (integer): number trajectories simulate condition. - `ref_mean` (numeric radian): baseline reference heading (default 0). - `concentration_base` (numeric): baseline von Mises concentration (kappa). - `concentration_slope` (numeric): optional slope applied predictor. - `tortuosity_base` (numeric): baseline angular noise scale. - `tortuosity_slope` (numeric): slope applied predictor. - `tortuosity_sd` (numeric): additional random variation per trial. - `predictor_mean`, `predictor_sd` (numeric): parameters used sample   per-trial predictor explicit values supplied. - `predictor_values` (list-column): optional explicit predictor values (length   `n_trials`) overriding generated values. predictor can represent continuous covariate (e.g. reference intensity). final heading concentration increases larger kappa, whereas larger tortuosity values produce sinuous paths.

what: Fit a von Mises distribution to per-group heading data — vonmises_fit
title: Fit a von Mises distribution to per-group heading data — vonmises_fit
text: Estimates mean direction \(\mu\) concentration \(\kappa\) von Mises distribution via maximum likelihood, together asymptotic standard errors confidence interval \(\mu\).  Intended parametric companion circ_dispersion: circ_dispersion returns empirical resultant length R circular SD, vonmises_fit returns MLE \(\hat{\kappa}\) uncertainty.

title: Fit a von Mises distribution to per-group heading data — vonmises_fit
code: vonmises_fit(hd, group_col = NULL, angle_col = "heading", conf = 0.95)

title: Fit a von Mises distribution to per-group heading data — vonmises_fit
text: hd Data frame containing headings radians. group_col Column(s) group .  NULL fits single model rows. angle_col Name heading column.  Default "heading". conf Confidence level interval \(\mu\). Default 0.95.

title: Fit a von Mises distribution to per-group heading data — vonmises_fit
text: Data frame columns group_col (supplied), mu   (MLE mean direction, radians), mu_deg (degrees), kappa   (MLE concentration), se_mu, se_kappa (asymptotic standard   errors), ci_lo ci_hi (conf-level interval   \(\mu\), radians), n.

title: Fit a von Mises distribution to per-group heading data — vonmises_fit
text: \(\kappa = 0\) corresponds uniform distribution (preferred direction); larger values indicate increasing concentration.  confidence interval \(\mu\) uses normal approximation unreliable \(\kappa < 0.5\) small samples.

what: Fit a wrapped Cauchy distribution to per-group heading data — wrappedcauchy_fit
title: Fit a wrapped Cauchy distribution to per-group heading data — wrappedcauchy_fit
text: Estimates mean direction \(\mu\) concentration \(\rho\) wrapped Cauchy distribution via maximum likelihood.  wrapped Cauchy heavier tails von Mises appropriate data outliers, weak noisy directionality, von Mises fit looks visually poor rose diagram.

code: wrappedcauchy_fit(hd, group_col = NULL, angle_col = "heading")

title: Fit a wrapped Cauchy distribution to per-group heading data — wrappedcauchy_fit
text: hd Data frame containing headings radians. group_col Column(s) group .  NULL fits single model. angle_col Name heading column.  Default "heading".

title: Fit a wrapped Cauchy distribution to per-group heading data — wrappedcauchy_fit
text: Data frame columns group_col (supplied), mu   (MLE mean direction, radians), mu_deg (degrees), rho   (concentration, 0–1), convergence (0 = converged), n.

title: Fit a wrapped Cauchy distribution to per-group heading data — wrappedcauchy_fit
text: \(\rho = 0\) uniform distribution (preferred direction); \(\rho = 1\) point mass (perfect concentration).  Unlike von Mises \(\kappa\), wrapped Cauchy \(\rho\) bounded \([0, 1)\). Standard errors computed mle.wrappedcauchy; check convergence optim return code (0 = fully converged; 1 = iteration limit reached estimates typically still reliable).  uncertainty estimation use vonmises_fit data compare model fits visually via add_vonmises_density add_wrappedcauchy_density.

what: Dwell-time proportions across quadrant x ring zones — zone_dwell
title: Dwell-time proportions across quadrant x ring zones — zone_dwell
text: Classifies trajectory observation one N quadrant sectors M annular rings, returns per-trial frame counts proportions. Applicable circular arena experiment spatial dwell time interest (water maze, open-field, Drosophila preference assay, etc.).

title: Dwell-time proportions across quadrant x ring zones — zone_dwell
code: zone_dwell(   x,   target_angle,   target_radius = 1,   ring_breaks = c(0, 0.5, 0.8, 1),   coords = c("absolute", "relative") )

title: Dwell-time proportions across quadrant x ring zones — zone_dwell
text: x [`TrajSet`] object x/y (rel_x/rel_y) columns registered. target_angle Numeric. Radians. Direction target zone arena centre. Q1 spans +/-45degrees around angle. target_radius Numeric. Accepted API symmetry [count_goal_entries()] used zone assignment. Default `1`. ring_breaks Numeric vector. Annular ring boundaries, must start `0`. Default `c(0, 0.5, 0.8, 1)` gives three rings: inner / middle / outer (thigmotaxis). coords Character. `"absolute"` (default) uses `@cols$x`/`@cols$y`; `"relative"` uses `@cols$rel_x`/`@cols$rel_y`.

title: Dwell-time proportions across quadrant x ring zones — zone_dwell
text: `data.frame` one row per observed (id x quadrant x ring)   combination, columns `id`, `quadrant` (integer, 1 = target),   `ring` (integer, 1 = innermost), `zone` (e.g. `"Q1.R3"`), `n_frames`   (integer), `proportion` (numeric). Combinations zero observations   omitted.

title: Dwell-time proportions across quadrant x ring zones — zone_dwell
text: target quadrant (Q1) centred `target_angle`; Q2–Q4 follow counter-clockwise. Observations outside `max(ring_breaks)` excluded counts proportion denominator.

title: Dwell-time proportions across quadrant x ring zones — zone_dwell

title: radiatR 0.2.0
text: Dialect-based loader system covering 20+ tracking tools. New extended dialects include TRex (positional CSV, #wcentroid / #centroid / #pcentroid variants, (cm) / (px) unit annotations), -maze, Tracktor, Ctrax (.mat via R.matlab, ellipse theta//b), TrackMate, idtracker.ai, ToxTrac, BORIS, dtrack. Pose-estimation support DeepLabCut SLEAP: load single bodypart likelihood-weighted centroid several, per-bodypart columns retained bodypart_axis heading rule. Multi-zone support EthoVision -maze (nose/tail/centre zone centroid single-zone selection). Register custom formats runtime register_loader_dialect(). Bundled single-file examples (inst/extdata/) DeepLabCut, SLEAP, Tracktor every dialect can tried without external data.

title: radiatR 0.2.0
text: pose_to_headings() derives many per-frame headings multi-point pose data — suited tethered-animal / gaze-direction designs. Additional heading rules registered register_heading_rule(), including bodypart_axis ellipse_axis.

Hypothesis tests returning tidy data frames, multiple-comparison correction via p_adjust: test_uniformity() (Rayleigh / Kuiper / Rao / Watson), test_mean_directions() (Watson-Williams, omnibus pairwise), test_concentration().
